body_txt: A mature, or at the very least late teenage, character, usually competent and assertive in public and at work, is shown to still sleep with a teddy bear, or a similar stuffed toy/animal. Typically, they're clutching it tightly to their chest while deep in slumber. If caught doing so, expect the teddy bear to be quickly shoved out of sight under the blankets, or a Death Glare to be delivered to the person catching them to let them know it's a good idea to just ignore it completely. Occasionally, however, no embarrassment or discomfort at all is shown upon being found with it. This can be used to show that the trappings of childhood are sometimes not easily left behind, or that a tough facade masks inner insecurity, or to make them more relatable to the audience by showing they've got a softer side. Bonus points if the character in question is portrayed as a tough, competent, no-nonsense individual who is more likely to sleep with a gun or a bottle of alcohol close at hand than a stuffed bear. Usually male, with Real Men Wear Pink in play. For multiple reasons, an adult female character who is shown to still sleep with a teddy bear raises far fewer eyebrows. Often times, a Manchild and/or One of the Kids will be this trope, to show their childish nature to the audience. Likewise, the Excited Kids' Show Host will proudly display their plushies to the audience, to demonstrate their eccentric nature and make them friendlier to the children watching. If the character is questioned about it, expect embarrassed stammering, with an eventual admission that they can't sleep without it or that it's a cherished childhood keepsake that they just can't bear (no pun intended) to part with. May be an Unmanly Secret that suddenly isn't so secret anymore. Compare Entertainment Below Their Age , Still Sucks Thumb , and Still Believes in Santa for other tropes about adults doing kiddie things. For some adults, this is Truth in Television . Studies have shown that some people who sleep with stuffed animals do so because it makes them feel less alone and reminds them of their childhood. Generally played for comedic effect, although there are exceptions that can make the entire situation very unsettling. Related to and a subtrope of Security Blanket .
